Argentine Flavor

Discover the best traditional recipes from our land.

Tucuman Empanadas

Authentic hand-cut meat empanadas. Juicy, made with flank steak, raisins, hard-boiled egg and olives.

⏱ 1h 30m 🔥 Medium
View full recipe &rarr;

Traditional Asado

The classic Argentine Sunday BBQ. Slowly grilled ribs, flank steak and sausages over wood fire. Served with chimichurri.

⏱ 3h 🔥 High
View full recipe &rarr;

Cornstarch Alfajores

Soft cornstarch cookies filled with thick dulce de leche and rolled in shredded coconut. Perfect with mate.

⏱ 1h 🔥 Low
View full recipe &rarr;

Napolitan Milanesa

The Buenos Aires legend. Breaded beef steak topped with tomato sauce, ham, melted mozzarella cheese and oregano.

⏱ 45m 🔥 Low
View full recipe &rarr;

Traditional Locro

A thick, hearty stew perfect for national holidays. Made with white corn, beans, squash, and various cuts of pork and beef.

⏱ 4h 🔥 High
View full recipe &rarr;

Argentine Choripan

Glorious grilled sausage sandwich inside crusty French bread, overflowing with fresh chimichurri sauce.

⏱ 30m 🔥 Very Low
View full recipe &rarr;

Mixed Flan Custard

A classic local dessert. Caramelized, soft and creamy custard, crowned with both dulce de leche and whipped cream.

⏱ 1h 30m 🔥 Medium
View full recipe &rarr;

Quince Pastelitos

Deep-fried puff pastries, crispy outside and juicy inside, filled with quince paste and glazed in light syrup.

⏱ 1h 🔥 Medium
View full recipe &rarr;

Steamed Humita

The traditional northern flavor. A thick cream of corn, squash and basil, cooked and wrapped inside fresh corn husks.

⏱ 2h 🔥 High
View full recipe &rarr;

Grilled Provoleta

A generous slice of provolone cheese grilled perfectly, boasting a golden crispy crust outside and melted core inside.

⏱ 15m 🔥 Low
View full recipe &rarr;

Made with ❤️ for good food lovers
